name: CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog
description: CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog — list of vulnerabilities known to be exploited in the wild. Federal agencies must remediate KEV entries by binding operational directive (BOD 22-01).
relationToCloudSecurity: Cloud workload protection platforms commonly integrate KEV for prioritized remediation.
relationToAISecurity: Applies to AI systems within scope.

license.notes: Government publication. Verify per-jurisdiction reuse terms.

current_extraction.notes: Reference-only by policy: KEV is published as a continuously-updated JSON feed at https://www.cisa.gov/sites/default/files/feeds/known_exploited_vulnerabilities.json. Per repo policy on upstream-hosted, version-tagged public data (see CLAUDE.md), we do not bulk-mirror — consumers pull directly from the CISA feed.

notes: Reference-only — see current_extraction.notes. CISA publishes KEV as a live JSON feed; mirroring it here would only drift from upstream.

desired_end_state.notes: CISA publishes the KEV catalog as a JSON feed daily-updated at cisa.gov/known-exploited-vulnerabilities-catalog and at github.com/cisagov/kev-data. Same upstream-only pattern as CVE/CWE — local bulk-mirror would drift; consumers should hit the upstream feed.
